The pleadings were incorrectly captioned, when they were AMENDMENTS to the [second] amended first account, nor merely supplements. (2) An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] (3) An estate still has not been opened. Conservatee's successor(s)-in-interest, who are entitled to notice, has/have not been determined. Deadline for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings is 10/13/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/24/25. (No earlier availability.) (1) No proof of service of a correct, complete notice has been filed yet, showing that notice, with a copy of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it, was mailed at least 30 days before the hearing on the fourth amended first account. (2) A "notice of the irrevocability" showing just the date of death of the trustor is not the proper notice required by Probate Code Section 16061.7. (3) The bank statements attached to the "Response" filed on 11/6/24 (a courtesy copy of which was never received by the assigned examiner) did not include a Schwab statement verifying the ending balance as of 12/31/22. (4) The trust documents filed on 8/7/25 (an incomplete copy was received by the examiner) indicate that the 2014 trust had a Schedule A attached to it, which included the real property that was transferred to the 1997 trust. The acting trustee has not addressed why the 2014 trust is not valid. She appears to have a conflict, as she has no incentive to argue that a later trust is the operative trust when she is neither the trustee nor a beneficiary of that later trust. Even if the 1997 trust was funded with the San Francisco real property, if the 2014 trust is valid, the trustee of the 2014 trust could petition under Probate Code Section 850 for a determination that that property should actually be an asset of the 2014 trust. Is Madeline Mulky aware that she is named as the trustee of the 2014 trust? Deadline is 11/10/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (This accounting will need to be adjudicated with the conservator's second/final accounting because of the reported transfers to the conservatorship estate in 2022.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD J. GIETZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 6/30/25 is incomplete at item 4a. (2) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Interest accrues on general pecuniary bequests not made within one year from the date of death. [Probate Code Sections 12003 and 12001.] (4) Distribution cannot be made to an "estate" of a post-deceased beneficiary; it must be to a person appointed as the personal representative or, if there is no formal proceeding, to the person(s) entitled under Probate Code Section 13100, if applicable. (What is Exhibit C supposed to support?) (5) There is a tab for Exhibit B but without any pages. (6) The Court waived notice to Decedent's cousin Richard Rogers but did not order that he is not entitled to distribution. If he has still not been located, his $50+ interest should be distributed to the county treasurer. [Probate Code Section 11850(a).] (7) Cash on hand is just $0.49 more than on the date of death nearly four years ago? (8) The prayer includes a request to reserve $5,000, but this is not disclosed in the petition and not accounted for in the proposed distribution. (9) The petitioner is NOT the primary HEIR. He, as the reported trustee of the devisee trust, is the sole beneficiary under the will. The sole heir was Decedent's brother Richard Gietzen. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/22/25. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDO J FRUCTUOSO JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. The amended petition was e-filed on 8/12/25, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) A 13100 affidavit must be conventionally filed. [LRSF 14.59 C.] (3) A complete 13100 affidavit must also include the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-300. (4) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (5) The unauthorized distribution was purportedly to Isabel Fructuoso and Ed Fructuoso, but one check was made to just Isabel Fructuoso. No receipt has been filed by Ed Fructuoso. (6) All of the funds in excess of the FDIC insured amount have been maintained in non-interest-bearing accounts at one bank, although the real property was sold in May 2024? (7) Attorney costs are not actually fully itemized: the first petition filing fee was $435, not $603.50. Many of the costs appear to be quite high; e-filing fees are usually under $10 per filing. Anticipated costs should be paid out of any reserve. There was no filing fee for this amended petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/13/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must BEGIN with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] (The accounting period ostensibly ends 8/1/25, but the bank statement, which should encompass, not precede the ending date of the accounting period, was for the period ending 7/31/25.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Extended Time
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Matthew DeRyan's Motion for Extended Time. Per the Notice of Motion, the motion is brought to obtain "an order for Extended Time regarding current "off docket" status designated for this case done by judge [sic] Moody on 6-23-2025 in department # 204." The Noice of Motion requests "a minimum of 90 days" based on Mr. DeRyan's "ADA asserted rights." Overall, despite its many and various allegations, the Motion fails to state a legally cognizable claim or claims. While it is clear in the Notice of Motion that Mr. DeRyan is seeking an order of the court extending a deadline "at least 90 days", it is unclear what deadline Mr. DeRyan believes he would be extending. The Declaration cites Cal. Rule of Court, rule 3.110 without analysis. Rule 3.110 is applicable to service of complaint, cross-complaint and response in civil actions. There is no explanation as to how it is relevant to this probate proceeding. Notwithstanding, the court extends the Mr. DeRyan an opportunity to appear and explain the relief he is seeking via this Motion, at the hearing scheduled for August 27, 2025 at 2:00 p.m. in Department 204 of this court, located at 400 McAllister Street, San Francisco CA 94102. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) The original will appears to have been lodged in Placer County. If a will has been delivered to the clerk of the superior court in which no proceeding is pending to administer the testator's estate, that court may order the will transferred to the clerk of the superior court in a county in which such a proceeding is pending (Probate Code Section 8203). 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY R. SCHMIDT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on filing the final Inventory and Appraisal is set for 2/2/26. No appearance will be necessary on that date if the final Inventory and Appraisal is filed at least two weeks prior. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 9/28/26. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANN MARCELLA FONTANELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing solely to admit the will. No appearance required.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY CHRISTINE OLESZEK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 03/22/2027.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/10/2025 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 08/21/2025). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 08/20/2025.
